headline: Minimum Wage Rise to RM1,700 Draws Mixed Reactions
context: The government announced a minimum wage increase from RM1,500 to RM1,700 effective January 2026, with SMEs given a 6-month grace period amid warnings of job losses and business closures.

cards.big: RM1,700 minimum wage still falls RM800 short of Bank Negara's living wage estimate of RM2,500
cards.sub: 13.3% increase sounds generous until you realise inflation eroded 11% of purchasing power since 2022

cards.big: SME Association warns 28,000 micro-enterprises employing fewer than 5 staff may close within 12 months
cards.sub: Labour costs account for 42% of SME operating expenses versus 28% for large corporations

cards.big: 3.2 million workers — 27% of workforce — earn at or near minimum wage across 14 sectors
cards.sub: Domestic workers, farm labourers, and retail staff in Kelantan and Perlis hit hardest by enforcement gaps

cards.big: Labour Department conducted only 12,400 inspections in 2024 covering 3% of 420,000 registered employers
cards.sub: Non-compliance penalties capped at RM10,000 per worker — too low to deter systematic wage theft

cards.big: A minimum wage nobody enforces is just a number on paper — not money in workers' pockets
Should Malaysia adopt regional minimum wages — higher in KL, lower in Perlis — to match local costs?
